name: Tony (Tongyun) Yang
tagline: mean the limits of the world?
in more than we can name.
openingHeadline: Five years deep in applied AI. Now I'm looking for the PhD that lets me go deeper.
openingBody: I have built and shipped work across world models, large language models, computer vision, and clinical AI, with collaborations spanning Tencent, Gradient Networks, MeetaVista, TU Delft, McGill, Tsinghua, and IMDEA. What I want next is the long-form, principled depth a PhD makes possible: research that is genuinely useful for the people who don't already have everything, and durable enough to keep at for a career.
currently: An independent AI researcher in Amsterdam. I currently collaborate with industry (Tencent, Gradient Networks, MeetaVista) and academia (TU Delft, McGill, Tsinghua) on cost-efficient large language models, optimization-as-reasoning, and intent-aware world models. Earlier roles include Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow at IMDEA Networks (Madrid), AI Research Engineer at TU Delft Imaging Physics, and an MSc in Computer & Embedded Systems Engineering at TU Delft.
intro: Open to PhD positions, ready to start any time. What pulls me is meaning more than topic.
researchThemesIntro: Two threads run through everything I work on. Pulling AI past the boundaries of language into perception and action, and bending those capabilities back toward fairness and access for the people they touch.

researchThemes.title: World models
researchThemes.blurb: Systems that perceive a scene and imagine what happens next. The bridge from describing the world to acting in it.

researchThemes.title: Large language models
researchThemes.blurb: Making capable models cheap, dependable, and useful enough to actually deploy. Routing, optimization, and agent design.

researchThemes.title: Computer vision
researchThemes.blurb: Vision as a channel for human signal, not just object detection. Reading emotion, intent, and attention from what people see.

researchThemes.title: AI for medicine
researchThemes.blurb: Models that work in the real clinic, not only on the leaderboard. Smaller, faster, and fair across patient populations.

researchThemes.title: AI safety
researchThemes.blurb: Building things that behave reliably when they leave the lab. Robustness, evaluation, and the unglamorous work of trust.

researchThemes.title: Fairness × access
researchThemes.blurb: A motivation, not a sub-field. Anything I build should reach the people who need it most, not the people who already have everything.

researchDek: What runs through every paper here. I want this work to be useful where it matters, accessible to people who don't already have it, and meaningful enough to keep at for years.
projectsIntro: Where the research meets real systems. Each one is a live collaboration with an actual partner (Gradient Networks, Tencent, MeetaVista). Most are still in motion.

businessCard.name: Tony Yang
businessCard.looking: Seeking PhD positions · ready any time
businessCard.tagline: World models · LLMs · vision · medical AI · safety

contactInvitation: I read everything. Reach out about research, collaboration, supervision, or just to say hi. I answer faster than is professional, and I bring my whole self to the conversation.
